stem: A line segment with endpoints (−2, 1) and (10, 6) in the xy-plane represents one of the legs of a right triangle. The area of this triangle is 78 square units. What is the length, in units, of the other leg of this triangle?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The length of the given leg is √((10−(−2))² + (6−1)²) = √(144+25) = √169 = 13. The area of a right triangle is (1/2)(leg1)(leg2), so 78 = (1/2)(13)(leg2), giving leg2 = 156/13 = 12.

Choice B doubles the correct answer. Choices C and D leave the radical unsimplified or use an incorrect length.
